stem: A decision-maker must choose among 3 drug treatment options. The probability of favorable patient genotype is uncertain, estimated between 33% and 43%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: Drug A: $274K in good patient genotype, $37K in bad patient genotype. Drug B: $65K in good patient genotype, $26K in bad patient genotype. Drug C: $93K in good patient genotype, $33K in bad patient genotype. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.38: best option is Drug A with EV=$127.1K. Drug A is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [115.2, 138.9].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
Drug A is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
